engineGuide.h2: Toyota Hilux Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Toyota Hilux across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 2.4L Diesel
entries.history: Replaced the 2KD in 2015, bringing improved efficiency and lower emissions to the Hilux range.
compatibleVariants: Active, Icon, Invincible, Double Cab, Single Cab, Extra Cab, Workmate
commonFailures: DPF clogging on short-journey vehicles (60k+ miles)
EGR valve carbon buildup affecting performance
Injector seal leaks causing fuel dilution
entries.cta: Get quotes for 2GD-FTV Toyota Hilux engine replacement

entries.title: 2.8L Diesel
entries.history: The flagship Hilux diesel, offering higher torque and power for demanding commercial use.
compatibleVariants: Invincible, Invincible X, GR Sport, Double Cab, Extra Cab
commonFailures: Injector tip wear causing rough running (80k+ miles)
Turbo actuator sticking on high-mileage units
EGR cooler leaks requiring replacement
entries.cta: Get quotes for 1GD-FTV Toyota Hilux engine replacement

entries.title: 2.5L Diesel
entries.history: The most common Hilux engine in the UK, known for durability but timing chain concerns.
compatibleVariants: Active, SR, Icon, Double Cab, Single Cab, Extra Cab, Workmate
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner failure (70k-100k miles)
Injector failure causing hard starting and smoke
Turbo wastegate sticking leading to power loss
entries.cta: Get quotes for 2KD-FTV Toyota Hilux engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Diesel
entries.history: The larger-capacity KD engine, favoured for towing and heavy commercial workloads.
compatibleVariants: Invincible, SR5, Double Cab, Extra Cab, Outback
commonFailures: Turbo wastegate actuator failure (common at 80k+ miles)
Injector seat erosion causing compression loss
Oil pump wear leading to low pressure warnings
entries.cta: Get quotes for 1KD-FTV Toyota Hilux engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Diesel
entries.history: Naturally aspirated workhorse engine, simple and robust but low on power.
compatibleVariants: Base, DX, SR, Single Cab, Double Cab
commonFailures: Oil pump wear causing low pressure at high mileage
Head gasket failure due to overheating
Injector nozzle wear affecting fuel economy
entries.cta: Get quotes for 5L-E Toyota Hilux engine replacement

entries.title: 2.4L Turbo Diesel
entries.history: Early turbo diesel option, now rare but still found on older fleet vehicles.
compatibleVariants: DX, SR, Single Cab commercial variants
commonFailures: Turbocharger bearing failure due to oil starvation
Injector pump wear causing hard starting
Timing belt failure if not replaced on schedule
entries.cta: Get quotes for 2L-TE Toyota Hilux engine replacement

entries.title: 2.7L Petrol
entries.history: Reliable naturally aspirated petrol engine, popular where diesel isn't suitable.
compatibleVariants: Active, Icon, Double Cab, Single Cab (select markets)
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner wear causing rattles (90k+ miles)
VVT-i solenoid clogging affecting performance
Oil consumption on high-mileage units
entries.cta: Get quotes for 2TR-FE Toyota Hilux engine replacement

entries.title: 4.0L Petrol
entries.history: Powerful V6 option for export markets and specialist UK imports.
commonFailures: VVT-i actuator failure causing timing issues
Oil sludge buildup if service intervals extended
Head gasket failure on overheated units
entries.cta: Get quotes for 1GR-FE Toyota Hilux engine replacement

entries.title: 2.7L Petrol
entries.history: Simple, robust petrol engine from the 6th generation Hilux.
commonFailures: Timing belt failure if not replaced at 60k miles

commonProblems.h2: Toyota Hilux Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Toyota Hilux is renowned for durability, but high-mileage commercial use exposes weaknesses in the 2KD-FTV, 1KD-FTV, and 2GD-FTV diesel engines. Timing chain stretch, injector failure, and turbo wastegate issues dominate replacement enquiries. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: Timing Chain Failure - Why 2KD-FTV Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Toyota Hilux Active, Icon, SR, Double Cab (2005-2015, 2KD-FTV 2.5L D-4D)
problems.rootCause: Hydraulic tensioner wear allows chain slack, causing rattles and potential jump.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace chain, guides, tensioner before failure
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught early before damage; 40k+ mile extension

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Cylinder head refurb, valve work if chain jumped
repairOptions.longevity: Only if no piston/liner damage; risk of hidden issues

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 2KD-FTV with upgraded tensioner
repairOptions.longevity: Complete solution; 12-month warranty; addresses root cause

problems.recommendation: On a Toyota Hilux worth £8,000-£15,000, preventative chain replacement at 60k miles is cost-effective. If the chain has jumped causing valve damage, a rebuilt engine with upgraded components offers better long-term value than extensive repairs.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 2KD-FTV engine prices for your Toyota Hilux

problems.h4: Injector Failure - Common Rail D-4D Problems
problems.rootCause: High-pressure injector tips wear, causing poor atomisation, hard starting, and smoke.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace one faulty injector, code to ECU
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix; other injectors likely to follow soon

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace all injectors, pressure test fuel system
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if engine otherwise healthy; 30k+ mile extension

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Rebuilt engine with new injectors, upgraded seals
repairOptions.longevity: Best value if cylinder damage present; warranty included

problems.recommendation: If only one injector has failed and the engine runs smoothly otherwise, replacing the full set prevents repeat failures. If injector failure caused cylinder scoring or piston damage, a rebuilt engine is the only reliable long-term solution.
problems.cta: Get quotes for D-4D injector replacement or rebuilt Toyota Hilux engine

problems.h4: Turbo Wastegate Failure - Loss of Power Issues
problems.affectedModels: Toyota Hilux Invincible, SR5, Double Cab (2005-2015, 1KD-FTV 3.0L D-4D)
problems.rootCause: Wastegate actuator sticks or linkage wears, causing over/under-boost and limp mode.

repairOptions.longevity: Quick fix if turbo internals undamaged; 20k+ mile extension
Viable if housing intact; requires specialist equipment

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Rebuilt 1KD-FTV with new turbo, upgraded components
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if turbo failure caused debris ingestion

problems.recommendation: If the turbo failed due to actuator wear alone and no debris entered the engine, actuator replacement or turbo rebuild is cost-effective. If compressor wheel damage sent metal into the intercooler/engine, a rebuilt engine prevents repeat failures.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 1KD-FTV engine prices for your Toyota Hilux

problems.rootCause: Short-journey use prevents DPF regeneration; EGR valves carbon up, restricting airflow.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Off-vehicle DPF clean, EGR valve clean/replacement
repairOptions.longevity: Effective if caught early; requires changed usage patterns

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: New/reconditioned DPF, EGR blanking if legal
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if engine otherwise healthy; check local regulations

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Rebuilt GD-series engine with emissions components
repairOptions.longevity: Best if DPF failure caused excessive backpressure damage

problems.recommendation: For commercial Hilux doing short trips, preventative DPF cleaning every 40k miles extends life. If DPF failure caused turbo or engine damage from excessive backpressure, a rebuilt engine with upgraded components is the reliable solution.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Toyota Hilux D-4D engine replacement with warranty

problems.h4: Oil Pump Wear - Low Pressure Failures
problems.rootCause: Oil pump gears wear, reducing pressure and causing bearing damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace oil pump, seals, flush system
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if caught before bearing damage occurs
Viable if crank undamaged; requires precision machining

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Rebuilt 5L-E/2L-TE with new pump, bearings, seals
repairOptions.longevity: Most reliable option for high-mileage commercial vehicles

problems.recommendation: On older Hilux with 100k+ miles, low oil pressure usually indicates bearing wear beyond simple pump replacement. A rebuilt engine offers better value and reliability than extensive bottom-end repairs.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt legacy Toyota Hilux diesel engine prices

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Toyota Hilux
finalCta.paragraph: Whether you're facing timing chain worries on a 2KD-FTV, injector issues on a 1GD-FTV, or turbo problems on a 1KD-FTV, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Toyota Hilux specialists. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, plus a 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare prices and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Toyota Hilux Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Toyota Hilux and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Toyota Hilux Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Toyota Hilux is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the correct replacement.

items.title: Toyota Hilux 2.4L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.4L D-4D (2GD-FTV) replaced the older 2.5L unit in 2015, bringing improved efficiency and lower emissions to the 8th generation Hilux while maintaining commercial-grade durability for demanding UK workloads.
items.cta: Get quotes for Toyota Hilux 2.4L diesel engines

items.title: Toyota Hilux 2.5L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.5L D-4D (2KD-FTV) powered the 7th generation Hilux and remains the most common engine in the UK fleet, valued for simplicity but known for timing chain tensioner concerns at higher mileage.
items.cta: Get quotes for Toyota Hilux 2.5L diesel engines

items.title: Toyota Hilux 2.8L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.8L D-4D (1GD-FTV) serves as the flagship diesel for the modern Hilux, offering higher torque for towing and heavy commercial use while incorporating updated emissions technology.
items.cta: Get quotes for Toyota Hilux 2.8L diesel engines

items.title: Toyota Hilux 3.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 3.0L D-4D (1KD-FTV) powered the 7th generation Hilux for demanding commercial applications, offering robust torque but requiring attention to turbo wastegate and oil pump maintenance.
items.cta: Get quotes for Toyota Hilux 3.0L diesel engines

items.title: Toyota Hilux 3.0L Legacy Diesel Engines
items.description: The naturally aspirated 3.0L (5L-E) powered early Hilux models with simple, robust design but low power output, now found primarily on older fleet and agricultural vehicles.
items.cta: Get quotes for Toyota Hilux 3.0L legacy diesel engines

items.title: Toyota Hilux 2.7L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.7L VVT-i (2TR-FE) provides a reliable petrol alternative for Hilux buyers avoiding diesel emissions complexity, with smooth power delivery and proven longevity when properly maintained.
items.cta: Compare Toyota Hilux 2.7L petrol engine prices

items.title: Toyota Hilux 4.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 4.0L V6 (1GR-FE) offered substantial power for export-market Hilux variants and specialist UK imports, though rarity means replacement parts require careful sourcing.
commonFailurePoints: oil sludge buildup if service intervals extended
items.cta: Compare Toyota Hilux 4.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Toyota Hilux 2.7L Legacy Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.7L (3RZ-FE) powered 6th generation Hilux models with simple, robust design but requires strict timing belt maintenance to avoid catastrophic failure.
commonFailurePoints: Timing belt failure if not replaced at 60k miles
items.cta: Compare Toyota Hilux 2.7L legacy petrol engine prices

fuelTypes.h2: Toyota Hilux Engines by Fuel Type
fuelTypes.intro: Choose your Toyota Hilux engine by fuel type to compare realistic replacement prices, known issues, and compatible UK supply options - from early 2.5 D-4D to the latest 2.8 D-4D.

items.title: Toyota Hilux Diesel Engines
items.description: Diesel dominates the UK Hilux range across all generations, with D-4D common rail technology delivering the torque and efficiency commercial users demand.
items.descriptor: Diesel dominates the UK Hilux range across all generations, with D-4D common rail technology delivering the torque and efficiency commercial users demand.
items.familiesLabel: Common Toyota Hilux Diesel Engine Families
families: 2GD-FTV - 2.4L D-4D (2015+), most common replacement engine, DPF/EGR issues
1GD-FTV - 2.8L D-4D (2015+), flagship torque, injector seal failures
2KD-FTV - 2.5L D-4D (2005-2015), high-volume unit, timing chain concerns
1KD-FTV - 3.0L D-4D (2005-2015), heavy-duty option, turbo wastegate failures
knownFor: Strong low-end torque ideal for towing and commercial loads
Timing chain tensioner wear on 2KD-FTV between 70k-100k miles
DPF clogging on short-journey 2GD-FTV/1GD-FTV units
High UK replacement demand on Active, Icon and Invincible variants
items.cta: Get quotes for Toyota Hilux Diesel replacement engines

items.title: Toyota Hilux Petrol Engines
items.description: Petrol Hilux variants offer simpler emissions compliance and smoother refinement, though lower torque makes them less common in commercial UK fleets.
items.descriptor: Petrol Hilux variants offer simpler emissions compliance and smoother refinement, though lower torque makes them less common in commercial UK fleets.
items.familiesLabel: Common Toyota Hilux Petrol Engine Families
families: 2TR-FE - 2.7L VVT-i (2005+), reliable naturally aspirated, timing chain wear
1GR-FE - 4.0L V6 (2005-2015), high-power import variant, VVT-i actuator issues
3RZ-FE - 2.7L legacy (1997-2005), simple design, timing belt critical
knownFor: Smoother, quieter operation than diesel equivalents
Timing chain tensioner wear on 2TR-FE at 90k+ miles
Lower fuel economy but simpler emissions system maintenance
Popular with private owners avoiding diesel complexity
items.cta: Compare Toyota Hilux Petrol engine prices

engineYears.h2: Toyota Hilux Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Toyota Hilux production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The 7th generation Hilux launched with updated D-4D diesel engines and improved commercial capability for UK fleets.
keyChanges: New platform with improved crash safety and cab design
Enhanced 4x4 systems with improved off-road capability
knownFor: Robust ladder-frame chassis suited to UK commercial use
2KD-FTV timing chain tensioner wear emerging at higher mileage
Strong residual values supporting replacement investment
Simple mechanical design aiding independent specialist repairs
years.cta: Get quotes for 2005-2011 Toyota Hilux replacement engines
years.ticker: Toyota Hilux 2.5 D-4D engine replacement • Toyota Hilux 3.0 D-4D timing chain • Toyota Hilux injector failure cost

years.description: Mid-cycle updates refined the 7th gen Hilux with minor styling changes and emissions improvements ahead of Euro 6.
keyChanges: Updated front grille and interior trim for refreshed appearance
Emissions tweaks to 2KD-FTV/1KD-FTV for Euro 5 compliance
Introduction of optional automatic transmissions on more variants
Enhanced infotainment and driver assistance features
knownFor: Mature platform with known reliability patterns aiding diagnosis
Increasing injector failure reports on high-mileage 2KD-FTV units
Strong parts availability supporting cost-effective repairs
Growing demand for supply & fit services from commercial operators

faq.h2: Toyota Hilux Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Toyota Hilux engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Typical rebuilt engine costs range from £1,200 for legacy 2.5L units to £4,200 for modern 2.8L D-4D, depending on variant, engine code and supplier. Supply-only pricing excludes fitting labour.
keyPoints: 2KD-FTV 2.5L rebuilt: £1,200-£2,400 supply only
2GD-FTV 2.4L rebuilt: £1,850-£3,600 supply only
1GD-FTV 2.8L rebuilt: £2,100-£4,200 supply only
Supply & fit adds £450-£950 depending on location
All prices include 12-month unlimited mileage warranty
items.cta: Compare Toyota Hilux engine replacement prices from UK specialists

items.question: What is the most reliable Toyota Hilux engine?
items.answer: The 2TR-FE 2.7L petrol is generally considered most reliable due to simpler design, though diesel 2GD-FTV offers better torque for commercial use when properly maintained.
keyPoints: 2TR-FE petrol: fewer emissions components, lower failure complexity
2GD-FTV diesel: robust when DPF/EGR maintained on longer journeys
Avoid short-journey use on DPF-equipped diesels to prevent clogging
Regular oil changes critical for timing chain longevity on all variants
High-mileage commercial use accelerates wear on all engine types
items.cta: Find a reconditioned Toyota Hilux engine with warranty coverage

items.question: Will a used Toyota Hilux engine fit my vehicle?
items.answer: Compatibility depends on exact engine code, transmission type and model year - always verify via registration lookup before purchase to avoid costly fitment issues.
keyPoints: Engine codes must match exactly (e.g., 2KD-FTV to 2KD-FTV)
Manual/automatic transmission affects bellhousing compatibility
4x4 vs 4x2 variants may have different mounting points
Emissions equipment varies by year affecting ECU compatibility
DVLA registration check confirms exact specification instantly
items.cta: Get quotes for Toyota Hilux engine replacement with compatibility guarantee

items.question: Is it worth replacing a Toyota Hilux engine or buying a new vehicle?
items.answer: For Hilux valued over £8,000 with otherwise good condition, rebuilt engine replacement typically offers better value than vehicle replacement, especially with warranty coverage.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engine cost: £1,200-£4,200 vs new Hilux £30,000+
12-month warranty on rebuilt units reduces financial risk
Commercial vehicles often have remaining chassis/body life
Consider downtime: engine swap typically 2-3 days vs new vehicle lead time
Factor in known issues on replacement vehicle when comparing options
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Toyota Hilux engine prices to inform your decision

items.question: How long does Toyota Hilux engine replacement take?
items.answer: Supply-only engines typically dispatch within 24-48 hours; supply & fit installations require 2-3 working days depending on garage workload and parts availability.
keyPoints: Next-day delivery available on many rebuilt engine options
Supply & fit appointments often require 48-hour advance booking
Complex variants (4x4, automatic) may need additional calibration time
Post-installation road test and ECU adaptation included in professional fits
Commercial operators can request priority scheduling to minimise downtime
items.cta: Find Toyota Hilux engine supply & fit deals with fast turnaround

items.question: What warranty comes with a Toyota Hilux rebuilt engine?
items.answer: All rebuilt engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty covering parts and labour for manufacturing defects.
keyPoints: Warranty covers internal components, gaskets and seals
Labour for warranty claims typically covered at fitting garage
Excludes damage from incorrect installation or maintenance neglect
Some suppliers offer extended warranty options at additional cost
Always confirm exact terms with chosen supplier before commitment
items.cta: Get rebuilt Toyota Hilux engine quotes with warranty details

items.question: Can I replace my Toyota Hilux engine myself?
items.answer: While technically possible for experienced mechanics, professional installation is strongly recommended to preserve warranty coverage and ensure correct calibration.
keyPoints: Modern Hilux engines require ECU adaptation after installation
Incorrect torque specifications can cause premature failure
Professional fit includes fluids, belts and post-installation diagnostics
DIY installation typically voids supplier warranty coverage
Supply-only option available for qualified independent garages
items.cta: Compare Toyota Hilux engine prices for supply-only or supply & fit

items.question: What causes Toyota Hilux D-4D engines to fail?
items.answer: Injector failure, timing chain wear and turbo wastegate issues are the most common failure points on 2KD-FTV, 1KD-FTV, 2GD-FTV and 1GD-FTV diesel engines.
keyPoints: Injector tip wear causes poor atomisation, smoke and hard starting
Timing chain tensioner wear leads to rattles and potential jump
Turbo wastegate sticking causes power loss and limp mode
DPF clogging on short-journey vehicles creates excessive backpressure
Regular servicing with correct oil specification extends component life
items.cta: Get quotes for Toyota Hilux D-4D engine replacement from specialists

items.question: Where can I buy a Toyota Hilux replacement engine in the UK?
items.answer: EngineMarket connects you with vetted UK specialists offering rebuilt, reconditioned and used Toyota Hilux engines with transparent pricing and warranty coverage.
keyPoints: Network includes 100+ specialist suppliers across the UK
Compare prices, warranty terms and supply & fit options side-by-side
All suppliers rated and reviewed by real Toyota Hilux owners
Registration lookup ensures exact compatibility before purchase
Secure enquiry process with no spam or pressure sales tactics
items.cta: Compare Toyota Hilux engine prices from trusted UK suppliers

items.question: How do I find my Toyota Hilux engine code?
items.answer: Engine codes are stamped on the engine block near the injection pump or on the vehicle identification plate - but registration lookup provides instant, accurate identification.
keyPoints: Common codes: 2KD-FTV, 1KD-FTV, 2GD-FTV, 1GD-FTV, 2TR-FE
Location varies by generation; consult owner's manual for exact position
Registration lookup via DVLA confirms code without physical inspection
Engine code essential for ordering correct replacement unit
Mismatched codes can cause fitment or ECU compatibility issues
items.cta: Enter your registration to identify your Toyota Hilux engine code instantly
